name: Kasha Cabbage Rolls
recipeIngredient: 1/2 tsp Mustard, dry, or to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Steam cabbage leaves until soft but easy to handle. Reserve 2 cups of cooking water.
2. Rinse and drain the kasha.
3. Bring 1 cup of reserved cooking water to a boil.
4. Add the kasha and onions to the boiling water. Return to a boil, then lower the heat and simmer for 20 minutes, or until all water is absorbed.
5. Add corn for the last three minutes of cooking.
6. Slice tempeh into strips (2 inches x 1/4 inch).
7. Fry tempeh strips in hot oil for about one minute on each side.
8. Mix the kasha mixture with fried tempeh strips and sauerkraut.
9. Place approximately 1/3 cup of filling in the center of each cabbage leaf, fold in the leaf's edges to seal the filling inside.
10. Place the rolls seam-side down in a casserole dish and keep warm until ready to serve.
11. Mix sauerkraut juice with mustard and remaining reserved water (1 cup) in a saucepan. Bring to a boil.
12. Dissolve kuzu powder in cold water, then stir into the hot juice mixture.
13. Simmer for 7 minutes, stirring constantly.
14. Pour the simmered mixture over the cabbage rolls and garnish with grated carrots.
15. Serve warm.
